place: Hope CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Hope was founded in 1769 by German Moravians who planned streets, homes, a school, a tavern, and a church before the village even existed.
The town’s name comes from the Moravians’ “hope of immortality,” a spiritual promise that guided their early community.
Settlers chose Hope for the reliable water power of Beaver Brook, which they believed would sustain mills and multiple businesses.
In the 19th century, railroads ran north and south of Hope while the Morris Canal flowed to the south, making the village a transportation hub.
Hope’s Moravian Cemetery and Hartung’s General Store were used as filming locations for two scenes in the horror classic Friday the 13th.
